name: Tetris
description: The universal classic. Highest score or most lines cleared wins. No explanation needed.

score_lo.notes: Score ones & tens digits (BCD). Source: libretro cheat15

score_mid.notes: Score hundreds & thousands digits (BCD). Source: libretro cheat16

score_hi.notes: Score ten-thousands & hundred-thousands digits (BCD). Source: libretro cheat17

lines_lo.notes: Total lines cleared — ones & tens. Source: libretro cheat26

lines_hi.notes: Total lines cleared — hundreds digit. Source: libretro cheat27

score_format.notes: 3 BCD bytes = 6 decimal digits. Max score: 999999

tournament_modes.name: Score Attack
tournament_modes.description: Highest score after a fixed time (5 min) or until game over wins

tournament_modes.name: Level Rush
tournament_modes.description: Highest level reached before game over wins

notes: Score stored as 3-byte BCD — decode: hi*10000 + mid*100 + lo (each byte = 2 BCD digits)
Universally known — zero learning curve for players
Score Attack on level 0 is the fairest competitive format
No lives mechanic — game ends when stack reaches top
fceumm is the standard accurate NES core
